_about: Checks excluded from the nightly vendored-spec run. No-silent-caps: every entry carries a reason, and this file is SHARED WITH THE VERIFICATION TEAM for agreement (their review condition 2-2) — an exclusion is a spec/seed gap to resolve, not a way to make the gate green. Keys: modules.<name> excludes a whole module; checks."<module>:<tc>" excludes one check. Categories used below: [retired-guide] the source guide no longer exists; [recheck-artifact] the spec deliberately preserves the ORIGINAL campaign guide expectation as fail-concordant for the team's recheck-gate — the underlying finding was since resolved doc-direction, so the live guide now matches the implementation and the old expectation is no longer a valid oracle (re-key requested); [env-coupling] the check pins identifiers that cannot exist on a fresh stack.

modules.gcp-deploy: [retired-guide] gcp-deploy.md was retired in #366 (described a nonexistent terraform/ tree; replaced by the Hetzner hosting page). Checks pin the retired guide's dataset shape (e.g. TC-GCPDEPLOY-06-002 expects exactly 5 demo projects; the agreed seed baseline adds fx-appr = 6). Spec retirement/re-target requested.

checks.integrations:TC-INTG-05-000-secret-setup: [env-coupling] Pins two project UUIDs from the original campaign DB; UUIDs are gen_random per stack. The seed provides the same fixtures by NAME (fx-appr github webhook + scan-pipeline gitlab slot, spec-pinned token) — re-key to name-resolve vars requested.
checks.audit-log:TC-AUDIT-03-004: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403 for developer audit read; 404 existence-hide is the deliberate convention (the spec's own note: '명세 404 정정 권고').
checks.audit-log:TC-AUDIT-04-003: [recheck-artifact] Encodes the case-sensitive action filter the original guide promised; L-12 resolved doc-direction in #369 (guide now documents ILIKE).
checks.audit-log:TC-AUDIT-05-002: [recheck-artifact] Expects page_size=1000 to return 200 per the original guide example; L-13 resolved doc-direction in #355 (guide now documents the 200 cap).
checks.disk-and-health:TC-HEALTH-06-002: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403 for developer admin-health read; 404 existence-hide is the deliberate convention.
checks.disk-and-health:TC-HEALTH-06-003: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403 for team_admin admin-health read; 404 existence-hide is the deliberate convention.
checks.integrations:TC-INTG-02-001: [recheck-artifact] Expects the API key to authenticate GET /v1/projects; L-19 resolved doc-direction in #355 (guide now points key auth at the enrolled endpoints).
checks.projects:TC-PROJ-10-006: [recheck-artifact] Expects team_admin to read GET /v1/admin/teams; M-11 resolved doc-direction in #356 (team_id is documented via /auth/me memberships).
checks.projects:TC-PROJ-13-003: [recheck-artifact] Expects a per-team unique index on project NAME; M-9 resolved doc-direction in #352 (slug is the documented unique key).
checks.sbom:TC-SBOM-07-002: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403 for non-member SBOM access; M-12 resolved doc-direction in #353 (404 existence-hide documented as intended).
checks.sbom:TC-SBOM-07-003: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403 for non-member NOTICE access; M-12 resolved doc-direction in #353.
checks.users-and-teams:TC-USER-01-003: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403 for developer /v1/admin/users; 404 existence-hide is the deliberate convention (spec note: '명세 404 정정 권고').
checks.users-and-teams:TC-USER-01-004: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403 for team_admin org-wide /v1/admin/users; same as TC-USER-01-003.
checks.users-and-teams:TC-USER-04-005: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403 for team_admin team creation; same existence-hide convention.
checks.vulnerability-data:TC-VDATA-05-003: [recheck-artifact] Expects 403; 404 existence-hide is the deliberate convention (spec note: '버그 아님, 명세 404 정정 권고').
checks.backup-and-restore:TC-BACKUP-06-005: [env-coupling] Expects an auto-YYYYMMDDT... backup in the list; auto backups are produced by the Celery beat midnight job, which a fresh nightly stack has never crossed. The workflow creates one MANUAL backup pre-run (satisfies TC-BACKUP-03-008's items[0] shape); the auto-name assertion stays environment-bound — re-key or a beat-trigger hook requested.
